chunk_summary: Guidelines for iron dosing in preterm/LBW/ELBW infants post-discharge, focusing on anemia prevention, dose adjustments, and transition planning at six months.
learning_objectives: Understand iron dosing recommendations for preterm/LBW/ELBW infants post-discharge.
Learn to calculate total daily iron exposure from medicinal and dietary sources.
Identify strategies to prevent anemia and avoid double dosing during the six-month transition.
Recognize the role of ferritin and hemoglobin monitoring in iron dose adjustments.
key_facts: Elemental iron dosing: 2–3 mg/kg/day for predominantly human-milk-fed infants starting at 2 weeks of age.
Higher-risk infants may require 2–4 mg/kg/day, with adjustments for EPO therapy or blood loss.
Iron-fortified formula intake must be factored into total daily iron exposure to prevent overdosing.
Medicinal iron is often needed for breastfeeding infants with low formula intake.
A single 'iron source plan' should be documented, including a transition date and stop/step-down rules at six months.
Monitoring ferritin and hemoglobin levels is recommended for dose adjustments when feasible.
reference_quote: A common outpatient error is continuing preterm iron dosing and adding national prophylaxis simultaneously at 6 months; prevent this by writing a single 'iron source plan' with a transition date and a stop/step-down rule tied to growth and (when feasible) hemoglobin and ferritin.
document_key_topics: Transition planning for iron supplementation at six months
Monitoring ferritin and hemoglobin for dose adjustments
